steps.title: Locate the main verb
steps.content: The sentence's main verb is "shows" ("Aslanian's macroanalysis... shows not only... but also..."). So the blank can't be another finite verb — that would create two main verbs in one clause.

steps.title: Pick the participle
steps.content:
We need a non-finite form (participle) to modify macroanalysis. "Examining" works as a present participle: "Aslanian's macroanalysis examining nearly 1,000 book titles... shows..."

(A), (C), and (D) are finite verbs and would conflict with "shows."

That matches choice B — B is correct.
